mnemonics: GIL Locks Threads (Global Interpreter Lock prevents multiple native threads from executing Python bytecodes at once, impacting multi-threading performance)
keyPoints: Python uses dynamic typing, where variable types are determined at runtime
List comprehensions provide a concise way to create lists based on existing iterables
The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) allows only one thread to execute Python bytecode at a time in CPython
commonMistakes: Modifying a list while iterating over it, which can cause unexpected behavior
Using mutable default arguments in function definitions (e.g., def func(arg=[]))
